book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.

classification.cautionReason: Conciliar or creedal material received in mainstream Nicene Christian tradition, with denominational differences on some canons.

lineage: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
title: Historical Introduction.

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: The history of the Council of Nice has been so often written by so many brilliant historians, from the time of its sitting down to to-day, that any historical notice of the causes leading to its assembling, or account of its proceedings, seems quite unnecessary.

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: The editor, however, ventures to call the attention of the reader to the fact that in this, as in every other of the Seven Ecumenical Councils, the question the Fathers considered was not what they supposed Holy Scripture might mean, nor what they, from à priori arguments, thought would be consistent with the mind of God, but something entirely different, to wit, what they had received.

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: They understood their position to be that of witnesses, not that of exegetes.

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: They recognized but one duty resting upon them in this respect—to hand down to other faithful men that good thing the Church had received according to the command of God.

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: The first requirement was not learning, but honesty.

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: The question they were called upon to answer was not, What do I think probable, or even certain, from Holy Scripture? but, What have I been taught, what has been intrusted to me to hand down to others?

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: When the time came, in the Fourth Council, to examine the Tome of Pope St. Leo, the question was not whether it could be proved to the satisfaction of the assembled fathers from Holy Scripture, but whether it was the traditional faith of the Church.

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: It was not the doctrine of Leo in the fifth century, but the doctrine of Peter in the first, and of the Church since then, that they desired to believe and to teach, and so, when they had studied the Tome, they cried out:

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: “This is the faith of the Fathers!

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: This is the faith of the Apostles!…Peter hath thus spoken by Leo!

verses.book: The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
The First Ecumenical Council: The First Council of Nice.
verses.text: No Acts of either of the first two Ecumenical Councils have been handed down.
